Touchpoint 01 — First Contact
01
The call that books —
or loses — a client forever.
More bookings from every enquiry
When someone calls and no one answers, they hang up and call the next place on Google. The first contact is everything.
7 sec
The average caller decides whether they feel welcomed within 7 seconds. If no one answers — they're already gone.
On the phone
  • Answered within 3 rings — every time, including evenings and weekends
  • Greeted warmly by name of the business — never just "hello?"
  • Sounds present and calm, not distracted or rushed
  • Questions answered confidently — no fumbling
  • Booking offered proactively, not waited for
On your website chat
  • Reply within 2 minutes — most visitors leave in 3
  • First message specific: "Are you looking to book, or do you have a question about one of our services?"
  • After hours — a human-feeling reply: "We're with clients right now — send your details and we'll be in touch first thing tomorrow."
The scripts
How to answer — always
"Good afternoon, [Studio Name] — this is [Name]. How can I help you today?"
When they want to book
"Absolutely — I'd love to get that sorted for you. What were you thinking of coming in for?"
After-hours voicemail
"Hi, you've reached [Studio Name]. We're closed right now — leave your name and number and we'll call you first thing tomorrow. Or visit [website] to book online anytime."
The real cost
The average beauty appointment is worth $80–$150. A loyal client visits 8–12 times a year. One missed call isn't $100 lost — it's potentially $1,200 a year, every year. Before referrals.
✦
How Pearl handles this
Pearl answers every call and every website enquiry — including after hours and weekends. She sounds warm, knows your services, and books the appointment in one conversation. No voicemail. No missed opportunity.
Touchpoint 02 — Confirmation
02
The message that makes them
feel they chose right.
Fewer cancellations, more anticipation
A warm confirmation doesn't just remind them — it reassures them they made the right choice before they've even walked in.
67%
of appointment cancellations happen because the client forgot — not because they changed their mind.
What great looks like
  • Sent within minutes of booking
  • Uses their first name and names the specific service
  • Tells them what to expect or how to prepare
  • Feels warm and personal — not like an automated receipt
  • Includes a simple way to reschedule without awkwardness
Booking confirmation
"Hi [Name] — you're all booked in for [Service] on [Day] at [Time] with [Name]. We can't wait to see you. If you need to reschedule, just reply here and we'll sort it. See you soon ✦"
For treatments — add a preparation note
"One thing to know — for the best results, come in with [clean skin / freshly washed hair]. We'll handle everything from there."
✦
How Pearl handles this
Pearl sends a warm, personalised confirmation the moment a booking is made — 24/7, automatically, in your voice.
Touchpoint 03 — No-Shows
03
Protect your slots.
Send great reminders.
Fewer empty slots, more revenue
Great reminders are a service to your clients, not a chase. And they protect your revenue before a no-show fee ever has to be charged.
$1,100
Average monthly revenue lost to no-shows at a single-location studio. One reminder sequence cuts this in half.
The timing that works
Reminder #1 — gives them time to reschedule, protecting your slot
48 hrs
Reminder #2 — catches genuine forgetters before it's too late
2 hrs
48 hours before
"Hi [Name] — just a reminder that you're in with us for [Service] on [Day] at [Time]. We're looking forward to seeing you. Need to move it? Just reply here."
2 hours before
"See you soon [Name] — your [Service] is at [Time] today. We're all ready for you. ✦"
On no-show fees
You're absolutely entitled to charge them. But clients who feel well looked-after and well-reminded almost never no-show. Do your due diligence first — then enforce your policy with confidence.
✦
How Pearl handles this
Pearl sends every reminder automatically, handles rescheduling replies, and notifies you the moment a slot opens up so you can fill it.
Touchpoint 04 — The Experience
04
The little touches that turn good
into unforgettable.
Word of mouth, social shares, clients who bring friends
The service is what they booked. The little things around it are what they talk about.
  • Offer a warm drink on arrival. Herbal tea, fruit water, a small coffee. Signals you've thought about their comfort before they sat down. Cost: ~$0.50 per client.
  • Greet them by name. "Hi [Name], welcome in — we're ready for you." One glance at the booking screen. Nobody forgets it.
  • Send them home with a sample. A tester matching what was used. Reminds them of you daily and often leads to a retail purchase.
  • Remember one personal detail. A holiday, a special occasion, a concern they mentioned. 20 seconds to note. Loyalty for life.
  • A handwritten note for first-timers. Left at their seat. Nobody expects it. That's exactly why it works.
  • End with intention. Walk them through what was done, what to do at home — then suggest the next booking.
What clients actually tell their friends
They don't say "the service was good." They tell stories. "They had my name ready." "She remembered my daughter's recital." "There was a handwritten note in my bag." Give them a story. That's your marketing — and it's free.
Touchpoint 05 — Reviews
05
The moment to ask.
And the ones
that sting.
More 5-star reviews, negative reviews turned to trust
Reviews are your most powerful marketing tool — and most businesses leave them entirely to chance.
92%
of people read online reviews before choosing a beauty business. Your Google rating is your most valuable front door.
The timing
The sweet spot is 2–4 hours after they leave. The results are fresh. They've looked in the mirror. They're still feeling it.
The ask — 2 to 4 hours after
"Hi [Name] — so lovely having you in today. If you have a moment, an honest Google review means the world to us. Here's the link: [link]. Thank you — it genuinely makes a difference. ✦"
Responding to a negative review — always publicly, always graciously
"Thank you for sharing this [Name] — we're genuinely sorry your experience wasn't what it should have been. Please reach out to us directly and we'd love to make it right."
Negative reviews are not the end
How you respond tells potential clients more about you than the review itself. A gracious, human response often converts readers into bookings. Respond to every single one — every time.
✦
How Pearl handles this
Pearl sends your review request automatically at exactly the right moment after every appointment — personalised, warm, with your direct Google link.
Touchpoint 06 — Retention
06
The follow-up that brings
them back before they drift.
Higher lifetime value, consistent revenue
Clients don't leave because they had a bad experience. They leave because life got busy and nobody reached out.
5x
cheaper to retain an existing client than to acquire a new one. The money is already in your client list.
Timing by service type
Haircuts / Colour
6–8 weeks
Lashes / Brows
3–4 weeks
Facials / Skin Treatments
4–6 weeks
Massage / Wellness
4–6 weeks
Medi Spa / Injectables
10–12 weeks
The rebook nudge
"Hi [Name] — it's been around [X weeks] since we saw you, and we'd love to get you back in. Want us to book your next [service]? Just reply here and we'll find you a time."
The "we miss you"
"Hi [Name] — it's been a little while and we miss you. We'd love to see you back. Reply here to lock in a time."
The best rebook moment
At the end of the appointment — before they leave. "Shall we lock in your next one now?" Train every team member to offer this every time.
✦
How Pearl handles this
Pearl tracks every client's last visit and automatically sends a warm rebook message at exactly the right interval for their service type.
Bonus One
Fill Your Books in Quiet Months.
Every business has slow weeks. The ones with full books send one message at exactly the right time.
The Welcome Back Offer
For clients who haven't visited in 8+ weeks. Send on a Tuesday morning.
"Hi [Name] — it's been a little while. We'd love to see you back. Book any appointment this week and we'll take 15% off as our welcome back. Just reply here: [link]"
The discount removes friction. The personal tone removes awkwardness.
The Flash Week
Scarcity + value + timing. Limit to the first 10 responses.
"[Month] is our quietest — so this week only, we're offering [Service] at [Price]. We have [X] spots. Reply BOOK to grab one."
The reason feels honest, not desperate. One-word replies convert better than clicking links.
The Treat Yourself Push
No discount needed. A reframe. Works beautifully in January.
"Everyone's focused on new year goals. We think the best one is taking care of yourself. January is our most peaceful month — book your reset: [link]"
Reframes "quiet" as a feature. Attracts your best long-term clients.
Bonus Two
Turn Clients Into Your Referral Engine.
Word of mouth is the number one way new clients find beauty businesses. Here's how to make it intentional.
The Bring a Friend Offer
Send to clients who've visited 3+ times.
"Hi [Name] — do you have a friend looking for a great [salon / spa / studio]? Bring them in this month and you both get [X off / a complimentary add-on]. We'd love to meet the people you love."
Your best clients know people just like them — and that's exactly who you want.
The Shareable Moment
Make your space worth photographing and sharing.
  • A beautiful quote on your mirror or wall
  • A small branded gift bag for first-timers
  • Your IG handle on every receipt and bag
  • A "ready to see?" reveal moment every time
One organic post from a client with 800 followers is worth more than a $50 ad.
The golden rule
"We'd love to meet the people you love" outperforms "refer a friend and get 10% off" every time — same offer, completely different feeling.
